chief_complaint: Persistent cough and difficulty breathing for 2 weeks
diagnosis: community-acquired pneumonia with underlying undiagnosed asthma

facts.content: Persistent productive cough, started dry and became productive with yellowish-green sputum over the past 5 days

attributes.progression: started dry, became productive 5 days ago

facts.content: Difficulty breathing and wheezing

attributes.severity: moderate, noticeable with walking and talking

facts.content: Low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), intermittent and worse in evenings
Night sweats, soaking through pajamas every night for 4 nights
facts.disclosure_condition: Student asks about constitutional symptoms beyond fever (e.g., chills, night sweats) OR asks specifically what happens at night besides the cough

facts.content: Right-sided chest pain, sharp, worse with deep breathing and coughing

attributes.characteristics: pleuritic, worse with inspiration and coughing

facts.disclosure_condition: Student asks about chest pain OR asks about pain associated with breathing or coughing

facts.content: Recurrent wheezing episodes since childhood (around age 8), occurring 2-3 times per year with colds or exercise, never formally diagnosed

attributes.duration: each episode lasts a few days
attributes.progression: has been happening for years, patient considers it normal

facts.disclosure_condition: Student asks whether breathing problems have occurred before this illness, OR asks about history of asthma, wheezing, or allergies

facts.content: Fatigue and malaise, has missed 3 days of work
Decreased appetite for the past 5 days
facts.disclosure_condition: Student asks about appetite, weight changes, or nutritional intake

facts.content: Seasonal allergies (allergic rhinitis) since teenage years, takes over-the-counter cetirizine as needed
No prior hospitalizations or surgeries
Cetirizine 10mg as needed during allergy season
Has been taking OTC dextromethorphan (cough suppressant) for the past week with minimal relief
Never smoked
Social alcohol use, 1-2 glasses of wine on weekends
No recreational drug use
Sexually active with her partner, uses oral contraceptives, no concerns
Several students in her class have been sick with upper respiratory infections in the past month
Lives with her partner and a cat; partner has had a mild cold recently
facts.disclosure_condition: Student asks about household contacts, sick contacts at home, OR asks about her living situation

facts.content: Mother has asthma
facts.disclosure_condition: Student asks about family history of respiratory disease, asthma, or lung conditions

facts.content: Maternal grandmother died of lung cancer
facts.disclosure_condition: Student asks about family history of cancer OR asks a general family history question and follows up on respiratory/lung conditions

facts.content: Elementary school teacher, works with young children daily
Lives in an apartment in Portland, Oregon with her partner
No recent travel outside the state
Last physical exam about a year ago, everything was normal
Allergic to penicillin — causes rash
